sanskrit_devanagari:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्त: श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति ।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥ २१ ॥
sanskrit_iast: yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥśraddhayārcitum icchatitasya tasyācalāṁ śraddhāṁtām eva vidadhāmy aham

translations.verbatim_quote: I am in everyone’s heart as the Supersoul. As soon as one desires to worship some demigod, I make his faith steady so that he can devote himself to that particular deity.
I am in everyone’s heart as the Supersoul. As soon as one desires to worship some demigod, I make his faith steady so that he can devote himself to that particular deity.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2932 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: God has given independence to everyone; therefore, if a person desires to have material enjoyment and wants very sincerely to have such facilities from the material demigods, the Supreme Lord, as Supersoul in everyone’s heart, understands and gives facilities to such persons.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.21 Yam yam, whichever; tanum, form of a deity; yah, any covetous person- among these people with desires; who, being endowed sraddhaya, with faith; and being a bhaktah, devotee; icchati, wants; arcitum, to worship; tam eva, that very; acalam, firm, steady; sraddham, faith; tasya, of his,
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-21-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Whichever form (tanum) a covetous devotee, prompted by his own nature, wishes to worship with faith — that same firm faith I (Krishna) hold up of his. The agency of the steady-faith is Krishna's, not the worshipper's; the worshipper is moved by his prior tendencies but the steadiness comes from the inner-witness.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.21 These divinities too constitute My body as taught in the Sruti text like: ‘He who, dwelling in the sun, whom the sun does not know, whose body is the sun’ (Br. U., 3.7.9).
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-21-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Those divinities also constitute Krishna's body (per the Br. U. śruti 'he who, dwelling in the sun, whom the sun does not know'). Even when the devotee does not know the deity is Krishna's body, Krishna takes the faith as directed to his own forms and makes it free from obstacles.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All four readings agree on the structural claim — Krishna is the firmness behind whichever faith the devotee adopts. The Advaita reading (Shankara) emphasises Krishna as the inner-witness that stabilises the faith; the Vishishtadvaita reading (Ramanuja) emphasises that the worshipped form itself is already a body of Krishna, so the worship is not other than worship of Krishna. Both readings agree that the agency of acalāṁ śraddhāṁ — the firm faith — is Krishna's. The engineering layer reflects the structural claim (the substrate makes the chosen technique's commitment firm) without claiming theistic substitution.

literal_meaning: Whatever form whatever devotee wishes to worship with faith, that very faith of his I make firm.
traditional_meaning_consensus: yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārcitum icchati / tasya tasyācalāṁ śraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmy aham — whichever form a devotee, impelled by his own nature, wishes to worship with faith, that very faith I (Krishna, the inner-witness) make immovable. Shankara: the worshipper turns toward whichever deity according to his prior tendencies; Krishna, present as inner Self, makes that very faith steady — the agency is Krishna's. Ramanuja: those divinities themselves constitute Krishna's body (Br. U. 3.7.9); the worshipper, even unaware that the divinity is Krishna's form, has his faith made steadfast by Krishna and proceeds without obstacle.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
